title: Review: wolftpm crates device/key/tss/shim
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — two P0 correctness bugs (shim response buffer check, test oracle), three P1 API/maintenance risks (error type, setenv duplication, closure type). No memory safety issues found.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: (1) shim response buffer limit may be intentional to prevent over-large allocations — worth confirming wolfTPM cmdBuf size in practice; (2) FMT1 stripping coverage — only the main strip path tested, not FMT1+P-flag combinations. Uncertainty driven by: cannot run tests without /dev/tpm0; cannot verify FMT1 behavior without a real TPM or a wolfTPM mock. Would raise confidence if: swtpm-based integration tests were run.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — no unsafe without SAFETY comments, no gratuitous clones, no index-loop anti-patterns. Two real idiom issues: Error::InvalidArg loses runtime values (pre-1.0 fixable), verify returning bool is a security footgun. No clippy-level issues found.
Rust idiom confidence: 10% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: verify bool-return is flagged as a design issue but current tpm-rs Connection::Error trait may constrain the design space. Uncertainty driven by: cannot see full tpm-rs trait requirements without running the build.
